skill.name: context-receipts
skill.description: Create privacy-safe audit receipts that prove how agent context was selected, deferred, loaded, pruned, compacted, or delegated.
skill.summary: Create privacy-safe receipts for agent context boundaries and context budget decisions.
skill.author: caioribeiroclw-pixel

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found markdown examples, JSON snippets, inline code, and relative documentation links rather than executable skill code. No prompt injection attempt, malicious intent, data exfiltration, or automatic command execution was found in README.md or SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: The static external-command matches occur inside markdown code fences, inline code, or JSON examples. They describe receipt verification and event formats, but the skill contains no script that automatically executes these commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown examples and static JSONL samples, not executable source files. No dynamic command construction or user-controlled shell invocation was found.

low_findings.title: Relative Example Paths Flagged as Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings are relative README links and example commands pointing to sibling example fixtures. They are documentation references, not file access logic inside the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only traversal-like strings are visible relative paths in README.md. The skill does not implement file reads, writes, deletion, or path normalization.

low_findings.title: Hash and Reconnaissance Terms Are Receipt Vocabulary
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto and reconnaissance matches refer to receipt metadata such as hashes, ids, counts, tool indexes, and audit gaps. These terms are used to avoid copying private content, not to inspect or compromise a host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is audit receipt design, with privacy-preserving hash placeholders and count fields. No weak cryptographic algorithm use or host reconnaissance command was found.

content.value_statement: Agent workflows can hide what context was loaded, deferred, or copied across boundaries. This skill provides privacy-safe receipt patterns that make those decisions reviewable without exposing private content.
actual_capabilities: Defines receipt fields for context selection, hydration, pruning, compaction, and delegation workflows.
Shows privacy defaults that exclude raw prompts, schemas, tool outputs, transcripts, secrets, and customer names.
Provides smoke-test patterns for MCP Tool Search, runtime tool-surface changes, and subagent tool exposure.
Documents receipt event names for skill loading, context attention, usage attribution, and rollback checks.
Uses hashes, counts, buckets, ids, and audit gaps to make review possible without copying sensitive content.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not generate receipts by itself.
It does not validate that an agent selected the best tool or produced a correct answer.
It depends on the agent or harness to emit receipt fields consistently.
The README references example fixtures outside the skill directory that may not be packaged with every installation.

use_cases.title: Review MCP Tool Loading
use_cases.description: Check whether an agent loaded compact tool indexes first, hydrated only selected tools, and kept raw schemas out of receipts.

use_cases.title: Audit Subagent Boundaries
use_cases.description: Verify whether child agent outputs, tool results, and MCP schemas crossed back into the parent context.

use_cases.title: Document Context Cleanup
use_cases.description: Record what pruning or compaction changed, what was protected, and what audit gap remains after cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Receipt
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the context-receipts skill to design a privacy-safe receipt for this workflow. Include selected context ids, copied raw content flags, token buckets, and audit gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Check Tool Search Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the context-receipts skill to verify Tool Search behavior. Show what was indexed, searched, hydrated, suppressed, called, and left unproven.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Subagent Delegation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the context-receipts skill to create a boundary receipt for a subagent task. Track delegated objective hashes, child output buckets, returned summaries, and raw output flags.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full Context Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the context-receipts skill to design receipts for retrieval, attention, Tool Search, pruning, compaction rollback, and usage attribution in one review plan.

output_examples.input: A team wants proof that Tool Search deferred most MCP schemas until one tool was selected.
output_examples.output: The result is a concise receipt plan listing index counts, selected tool id, hydration reason, suppressed definition count, raw schema flags, and remaining audit gap.

output_examples.input: A security reviewer needs to know whether a child agent copied raw tool output into the parent context.
output_examples.output: The result is a boundary receipt summary with delegated task category, child output size bucket, returned summary status, raw output flags, and privacy notes.

output_examples.input: A maintainer needs evidence that failed compaction did not replace the original context.
output_examples.output: The result is a transaction receipt checklist covering summary status, swap commit status, rollback restoration, replay checks, and authoritative context state.

best_practices: Use stable ids, hashes, counts, and buckets instead of raw private content.
Always include an audit_gap field that states what the receipt cannot prove.
Record before and after context budget buckets for pruning, compaction, and hydration events.
anti_patterns: Do not copy raw prompts, tool schemas, tool arguments, results, transcripts, secrets, or customer names into receipts.
Do not treat a receipt as proof that the agent chose the best tool or answer.
Do not emit vague statements such as Tool Search enabled without boundary evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a markdown guidance skill that describes receipt formats and review checks.

faq.question: What is a context receipt?
faq.answer: It is a small audit record that shows what context crossed a boundary and what stayed out.

faq.question: Can receipts include private prompts or tool outputs?
faq.answer: No. The skill recommends hashes, counts, ids, buckets, and boolean flags instead of raw private content.

faq.question: Does a receipt prove the agent was correct?
faq.answer: No. It proves boundary behavior and records the audit gap, but it does not prove semantic correctness.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when workflows select, defer, hydrate, prune, compact, delegate, or attribute context usage.
